description_long: This is an irregularly shaped, medium-sized stone with a rough, angular surface and a predominantly gray coloration. The stone features jagged edges and uneven planes, with some areas appearing slightly chipped or fractured, suggesting it has been broken from a larger mass. A small, circular white label with a handwritten marking is affixed to one side, indicating it may have been cataloged or studied. The surface texture is coarse and matte, with visible natural fissures and minor pitting. There are no visible signs of artificial modification or carving, and the stone appears solid and dense. Its overall appearance suggests it could serve as a natural obstacle or barrier in an urban park, garden, or roadside setting.
description: A rough, angular gray stone with jagged edges, coarse texture, and a small white label on one side. The surface is matte and uneven, with natural fractures and pitting.
description_view_0: This view shows the broadest face of the stone, highlighting its rough, uneven surface and the white label with a marking.
description_view_1: This side view reveals the stone's narrow profile and jagged vertical edges, emphasizing its thickness and angularity.
description_view_2: This angle displays another broad face with pronounced fractures and a more textured, pitted surface.
description_view_3: This view shows the stone's opposite narrow edge, with deep grooves and a darker appearance due to shadowing.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature or natural barrier on the ground
attribute_pattern: Irregular natural fractures and pitting, with no artificial carvings or regular veining; the only artificial mark is a small white label with writing.
attribute_weathering: Rough and angular with minor surface pitting and fractures, indicating moderate natural weathering but not extensive erosion.
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Typically blends with natural ground surfaces or landscaping elements, but the white label may cause slight visual contrast in manicured urban settings.
